title: Allow us to select the field that displays in the calendar widget
excerpt:

posts.author: Cyber_Lion (Sofia Rodriguez)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have spoken to Support and learned that there is no way to edit what field the Calendar Widget displays.

We have an Intake Form on Monday.com to manage a content calendar. Unfortunately, the Calendar Widget seems to arbitrarily select a field to display. In one test I created, it chose to display the email (the first question in the intake form), Thinking we could “hack” the desired behavior, I then created another Intake form and made the first question be the “Content Title”. In this second attempt, Monday.com created the Task column by default which then became the field that displayed on the calendar.

We can not successfully create a content calendar on Monday.com because of this lack of functionality. This seems like a very simple feature but without it, any social, marketing, events, content, or community team is unable to use the product for their workflows.

Screenshot 2025-02-17 at 7.45.22 PM1434×410 19.4 KB

Screenshot 2025-02-17 at 7.45.31 PM954×442 8.3 KB

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Sofia,

The only way I know how to fill the item name (“Task”), is to enable Include name question in form in the form’s settings. You can then rename the first question’s label to eg. “Content title”. Does that help?

image821×157 7.06 KB

If not, and you’re open to marketplace apps, Calendar Plus can display your content title as an extra field on calendar events, like so:

image477×183 6.27 KB

You can also display other pieces of information, like maybe the target social platform!

–

posts.author: Cyber_Lion (Sofia Rodriguez)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @reinier ! Appreciate the suggestions and glad to learn of a workaround that wasn’t shared via support.
